{{Infobox ancient site |name = Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun |image = [[File:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (with the front section on the right and the rear section in the back left) |Alternate name = |Group = {{ill|Mami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group) |location = {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Kitsui/Ryōfukuji area |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 32|Latitude seconds = 2.40 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 30.00 |ISO = JP-29 |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |Dimensions = Tumulus length: 140 m |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} |Artifacts = Steatite magatama beads with small beads inside, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments |Accompanying Mounds = |Construction Period = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Buried Individual = Possibly Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th) |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-NNE |Label = Kitsui Shiroyama
Kofun |Label Position = top }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group), it is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture. The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}} and {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and artifacts, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} and early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, corresponding to the middle to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal scale.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyogo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}} to {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid designated as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == Dimensions == The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * Tumulus length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Cultural Properties == === Related Cultural Properties === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyogo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kashiba]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:狐井城山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun |image = [[File:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (with the front section on the right and the rear section in the back left) |Alternate name = |Group = {{ill|Mami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group) |location = {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Kitsui/Ryōfukuji area |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 32|Latitude seconds = 2.40 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 30.00 |ISO = JP-29 |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |Dimensions = Tumulus length: 140 m |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} |Artifacts = Steatite magatama beads with small beads inside, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments |Accompanying Mounds = |Construction Period = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Buried Individual = Possibly Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th) |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-NNE |Label = Kitsui Shiroyama
Kofun |Label Position = top }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group), it is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture. The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}} and {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and artifacts, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} and early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, corresponding to the middle to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal scale.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyogo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}} to {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid designated as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == Dimensions == The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * Tumulus length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Cultural Properties == === Related Cultural Properties === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyogo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kashiba]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:狐井城山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name= Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun |image=[[File:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Right: anterior portion, left background: posterior circular portion) |Alternate name= |Group= {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group) |location=[[Nara Prefecture]], {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, Kitsui and Ryofukuji |Coordinates={{coord|34|32|2.40|N|135|42|30.00|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |Size=Mound length: 140 m |Burial structure=(Presumed) [[Nagamochi-shaped sarcophagus]] |Artifacts= Steatite magatama with smaller magatama inside, [[haniwa]] fragments |Sub-tombs= |Period= Late [[5th century]] to early [[6th century]] |Buried individual=(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} or {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} |Imperial tomb= |Historic site=None |Tangible cultural property= |Notes= |Map= Japan Nara |Icon= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NNE |Label= Kitsui
Shiroyama Kofun |Label position= top }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Kitsui and Ryofukuji, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is one of the burial mounds forming part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group). The kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture, this large keyhole-shaped kofun dates to the late [[Kofun period]]. It underwent modifications during the [[medieval period]] when it was used as a castle (Kitsui Fort). Excavations have been conducted in the surrounding embankment and moat areas. The mound is oriented with its anterior portion facing north and measures approximately 140 meters in length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Surface features include [[fukiishi]] and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and morning glory-shaped). A moat and an embankment encircle the mound. Although the burial facility is unclear, fragments of a carved {{ill|Nagamochi-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} have been found nearby, suggesting its use in the kofun. A steatite magatama containing smaller magatama was also discovered near the constricted neck of the mound. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[5th century]] to early [[6th century]] and is considered of "great king's tomb" scale. Hypotheses suggest that it could be the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} (23rd emperor) or {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} (25th emperor), both associated with the Katsushimo District.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|title=Kofun no Hisaisha wo Suiri Suru|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2018|pages=253-260}} Stone sarcophagus fragments found nearby were determined to be made from Ryuyama stone, a type of hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa basin in Hyogo Prefecture, differing from the tuff from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijo (Nara and Osaka)|ja|二上山}}.{{PDFlink|[https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Ryuyama Stone]}} (Hyogo Prefecture official website). North of this kofun is the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳}}, which some researchers identify as the northern tomb of Emperor Buretsu, complementing this kofun as Emperor Kenzo's southern tomb.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "Domestic Largest Steatite Magatama Discovered at Kitsui Inari Kofun"] (Nara Newspaper). == History == * [[Medieval period]] to [[early modern period]]: Used as a castle (Kitsui Fort) by the Okabe clan. * [[1970]] (Showa 45): Discovery of a sarcophagus lid stone in the Hatsuda River (now displayed at Futakami Cultural Center). * [[1981]]–[[1983]]: Excavation during embankment repairs by the {{ill|Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}. * [[1985]]: Excavation during embankment repairs by the Kashiba Town Board of Education. * [[1995]] (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid stone designated as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == Mound == The mound has the following dimensions:Historical Site Explanation Board, Kashiba City Board of Education. * Mound length: 140 meters * Posterior circular portion: Diameter approx. 90 meters * Anterior portion: Width approx. 110 meters The moat surrounding the mound measures 18 meters wide on the northern side. The outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high.{{Cite book|title=Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Related Cultural Properties === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sarcophagus lid stone in front of Futakami Cultural Center
(Kashiba City-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Nagamochi-shaped sarcophagus lid stone *: Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (Archaeological Material, Designated No. 8). Located in the front yard of the Futakami Cultural Center. *: Made of hyaloclastite (Ryuyama stone) from the Kakogawa basin in Hyogo Prefecture. Measures 270 cm in length, with decorative protrusions. Found in 1970 in the Hatsuda River and presumed to have been used in this kofun. Designated March 9, 1995.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Nagamochi-shaped sarcophagus lid stone]}} (Kashiba City official website). In the surrounding area, two additional sarcophagus lid stones and a ceiling stone fragment were discovered in 1969. These are preserved at the northern end of the Amida Bridge east side.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Sarcophagus lid stones and ceiling stone fragment]}} (Kashiba City official website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lid stone and ceiling stone at Amida Bridge (Kashiba City-designated Cultural Properties) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun Explanation Board (Kashiba City Board of Education, installed in 2010) * Sarcophagus Lid Stone Explanation Board (Kashiba City Board of Education, installed in 1995) * {{Cite book|author=Heibonsha|title=Nara Prefecture Placenames|series=Japanese Historical Placenames Series Vol. 30|year=1981}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|title=The Two Great King Tombs in Katsuragi|journal=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin|year=2011}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiba City]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] * [[Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]] Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara| [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:狐井城山古墳]] ... 7 KB (942 words) - Kofun |Label Position = top }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group), it is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture. The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}} and {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and artifacts, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} and early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, corresponding to the middle to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal scale.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyogo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}} to {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid designated as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == Dimensions == The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * Tumulus length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Cultural Properties == === Related Cultural Properties === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyogo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kashiba]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:狐井城山古墳]]

Mound (Right: anterior portion, left background: posterior circular portion) |Alternate name= |Group= {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group) |location=[[Nara Prefecture]], {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, Kitsui and Ryofukuji |Coordinates={{coord|34|32|2.40|N|135|42|30.00|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |Size=Mound length: 140 m |Burial structure=(Presumed) [[Nagamochi-shaped sarcophagus]] |Artifacts= Steatite magatama with smaller magatama inside, [[haniwa]] fragments |Sub-tombs= |Period= Late [[5th century]] to early [[6th century]] |Buried individual=(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} or {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} |Imperial tomb= |Historic site=None |Tangible cultural property= |Notes= |Map= Japan Nara |Icon= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NNE |Label= Kitsui
Shiroyama Kofun |Label position= top }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Kitsui and Ryofukuji, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is one of the burial mounds forming part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group). The kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture, this large keyhole-shaped kofun dates to the late [[Kofun period]]. It underwent modifications during the [[medieval period]] when it was used as a castle (Kitsui Fort). Excavations have been conducted in the surrounding embankment and moat areas. The mound is oriented with its anterior portion facing north and measures approximately 140 meters in length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Surface features include [[fukiishi]] and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and morning glory-shaped). A moat and an embankment encircle the mound. Although the burial facility is unclear, fragments of a carved {{ill|Nagamochi-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} have been found nearby, suggesting its use in the kofun. A steatite magatama containing smaller magatama was also discovered near the constricted neck of the mound. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[5th century]] to early [[6th century]] and is considered of "great king's tomb" scale. Hypotheses suggest that it could be the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} (23rd emperor) or {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} (25th emperor), both associated with the Katsushimo District.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|title=Kofun no Hisaisha wo Suiri Suru|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2018|pages=253-260}} Stone sarcophagus fragments found nearby were determined to be made from Ryuyama stone, a type of hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa basin in Hyogo Prefecture, differing from the tuff from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijo (Nara and Osaka)|ja|二上山}}.{{PDFlink|[https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Ryuyama Stone]}} (Hyogo Prefecture official website). North of this kofun is the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳}}, which some researchers identify as the northern tomb of Emperor Buretsu, complementing this kofun as Emperor Kenzo's southern tomb.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "Domestic Largest Steatite Magatama Discovered at Kitsui Inari Kofun"] (Nara Newspaper). == History == * [[Medieval period]] to [[early modern period]]: Used as a castle (Kitsui Fort) by the Okabe clan. * [[1970]] (Showa 45): Discovery of a sarcophagus lid stone in the Hatsuda River (now displayed at Futakami Cultural Center). * [[1981]]–[[1983]]: Excavation during embankment repairs by the {{ill|Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}. * [[1985]]: Excavation during embankment repairs by the Kashiba Town Board of Education. * [[1995]] (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid stone designated as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == Mound == The mound has the following dimensions:Historical Site Explanation Board, Kashiba City Board of Education. * Mound length: 140 meters * Posterior circular portion: Diameter approx. 90 meters * Anterior portion: Width approx. 110 meters The moat surrounding the mound measures 18 meters wide on the northern side. The outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high.{{Cite book|title=Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Related Cultural Properties === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sarcophagus lid stone in front of Futakami Cultural Center
(Kashiba City-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Nagamochi-shaped sarcophagus lid stone *: Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (Archaeological Material, Designated No. 8). Located in the front yard of the Futakami Cultural Center. *: Made of hyaloclastite (Ryuyama stone) from the Kakogawa basin in Hyogo Prefecture. Measures 270 cm in length, with decorative protrusions. Found in 1970 in the Hatsuda River and presumed to have been used in this kofun. Designated March 9, 1995.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Nagamochi-shaped sarcophagus lid stone]}} (Kashiba City official website). In the surrounding area, two additional sarcophagus lid stones and a ceiling stone fragment were discovered in 1969. These are preserved at the northern end of the Amida Bridge east side.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Sarcophagus lid stones and ceiling stone fragment]}} (Kashiba City official website). The burial is believed to be of a high-ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal scale.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyogo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}} to {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid designated as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == Dimensions == The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * Tumulus length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Cultural Properties == === Related Cultural Properties === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyogo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Cluster]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kashiba]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:狐井城山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun |image = [[File:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (with the front section on the right and the rear section in the back left) |Alternate name = |Group = {{ill|Mami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group) |location = {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Kitsui/Ryōfukuji area |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 32|Latitude seconds = 2.40 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 30.00 |ISO = JP-29 |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |Dimensions = Tumulus length: 140 m |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} |Artifacts = Steatite magatama beads with small beads inside, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments |Accompanying Mounds = |Construction Period = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Buried Individual = Possibly Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th) |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-NNE |Label = Kitsui Shiroyama
Kofun |Label Position = top }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group), it is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture. The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}} and {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and artifacts, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} and early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, corresponding to the middle to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal scale.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyogo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}} to {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid designated as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == Dimensions == The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * Tumulus length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Cultural Properties == === Related Cultural Properties === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyogo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Cluster]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kashiba]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:狐井城山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name= Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun |image=[[File:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Right: anterior portion, left background: posterior circular portion) |Alternate name= |Group= {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group) |location=[[Nara Prefecture]], {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, Kitsui and Ryofukuji |Coordinates={{coord|34|32|2.40|N|135|42|30.00|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |Size=Mound length: 140 m |Burial structure=(Presumed) [[Nagamochi-shaped sarcophagus]] |Artifacts= Steatite magatama with smaller magatama inside, [[haniwa]] fragments |Sub-tombs= |Period= Late [[5th century]] to early [[6th century]] |Buried individual=(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} or {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} |Imperial tomb= |Historic site=None |Tangible cultural property= |Notes= |Map= Japan Nara |Icon= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NNE |Label= Kitsui
Shiroyama Kofun |Label position= top }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Kitsui and Ryofukuji, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is one of the burial mounds forming part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group). The kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture, this large keyhole-shaped kofun dates to the late [[Kofun period]]. It underwent modifications during the [[medieval period]] when it was used as a castle (Kitsui Fort). Excavations have been conducted in the surrounding embankment and moat areas. The mound is oriented with its anterior portion facing north and measures approximately 140 meters in length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Surface features include [[fukiishi]] and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and morning glory-shaped). A moat and an embankment encircle the mound. Although the burial facility is unclear, fragments of a carved {{ill|Nagamochi-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} have been found nearby, suggesting its use in the kofun. A steatite magatama containing smaller magatama was also discovered near the constricted neck of the mound. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[5th century]] to early [[6th century]] and is considered of "great king's tomb" scale. Hypotheses suggest that it could be the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} (23rd emperor) or {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} (25th emperor), both associated with the Katsushimo District.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|title=Kofun no Hisaisha wo Suiri Suru|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2018|pages=253-260}} Stone sarcophagus fragments found nearby were determined to be made from Ryuyama stone, a type of hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa basin in Hyogo Prefecture, differing from the tuff from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijo (Nara and Osaka)|ja|二上山}}.{{PDFlink|[https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Ryuyama Stone]}} (Hyogo Prefecture official website). North of this kofun is the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳}}, which some researchers identify as the northern tomb of Emperor Buretsu, complementing this kofun as Emperor Kenzo's southern tomb.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "Domestic Largest Steatite Magatama Discovered at Kitsui Inari Kofun"] (Nara Newspaper). == History == * [[Medieval period]] to [[early modern period]]: Used as a castle (Kitsui Fort) by the Okabe clan. * [[1970]] (Showa 45): Discovery of a sarcophagus lid stone in the Hatsuda River (now displayed at Futakami Cultural Center). * [[1981]]–[[1983]]: Excavation during embankment repairs by the {{ill|Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}. * [[1985]]: Excavation during embankment repairs by the Kashiba Town Board of Education. * [[1995]] (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid stone designated as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == Mound == The mound has the following dimensions:Historical Site Explanation Board, Kashiba City Board of Education. * Mound length: 140 meters * Posterior circular portion: Diameter approx. 90 meters * Anterior portion: Width approx. 110 meters The moat surrounding the mound measures 18 meters wide on the northern side. The outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high.{{Cite book|title=Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Related Cultural Properties === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sarcophagus lid stone in front of Futakami Cultural Center
(Kashiba City-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Nagamochi-shaped sarcophagus lid stone *: Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (Archaeological Material, Designated No. 8). Located in the front yard of the Futakami Cultural Center. *: Made of hyaloclastite (Ryuyama stone) from the Kakogawa basin in Hyogo Prefecture. Measures 270 cm in length, with decorative protrusions. Found in 1970 in the Hatsuda River and presumed to have been used in this kofun. Designated March 9, 1995.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Nagamochi-shaped sarcophagus lid stone]}} (Kashiba City official website). In the surrounding area, two additional sarcophagus lid stones and a ceiling stone fragment were discovered in 1969. These are preserved at the northern end of the Amida Bridge east side.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Sarcophagus lid stones and ceiling stone fragment]}} (Kashiba City official website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lid stone and ceiling stone at Amida Bridge (Kashiba City-designated Cultural Properties) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun Explanation Board (Kashiba City Board of Education, installed in 2010) * Sarcophagus Lid Stone Explanation Board (Kashiba City Board of Education, installed in 1995) * {{Cite book|author=Heibonsha|title=Nara Prefecture Placenames|series=Japanese Historical Placenames Series Vol. 30|year=1981}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|title=The Two Great King Tombs in Katsuragi|journal=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin|year=2011}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiba City]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] * [[Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]] Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara| [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:狐井城山古墳]] ... 7 KB (942 words) - 04:26, 15 January[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 2025 * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳}} (Kashiba, Late Kofun Period, keyhole-shaped, total length 109 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] 2025 * [[Nagamochi-type sarcophagus]]''' {{ill|Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳}} – {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市}}, 140 meters ... 7 KB (783 words) - 23:59, 14 January[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] 2025

These are preserved at the northern end of the Amida Bridge east side.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Sarcophagus lid stones and ceiling stone fragment]}} (Kashiba City official website). {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun |image = [[File:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (with the front section on the right and the rear section in the back left) |Alternate name = |Group = {{ill|Mami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group) |location = {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Kitsui/Ryōfukuji area |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 32|Latitude seconds = 2.40 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 30.00 |ISO = JP-29 |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |Dimensions = Tumulus length: 140 m |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} |Artifacts = Steatite magatama beads with small beads inside, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments |Accompanying Mounds = |Construction Period = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Buried Individual = Possibly Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th) |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-NNE |Label = Kitsui Shiroyama
Kofun |Label Position = top }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group), it is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture. The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}} and {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and artifacts, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} and early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, corresponding to the middle to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal scale.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyogo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}} to {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid designated as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == Dimensions == The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * Tumulus length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Cultural Properties == === Related Cultural Properties === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyogo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Cluster]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kashiba]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:狐井城山古墳]]

Kofun |Label Position = top }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group), it is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture. The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}} and {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and artifacts, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} and early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, corresponding to the middle to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal scale.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyogo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}} to {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid designated as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == Dimensions == The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * Tumulus length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Cultural Properties == === Related Cultural Properties === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyogo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Mami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Kashiba]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:狐井城山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name= Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun |image=[[File:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Right: anterior portion, left background: posterior circular portion) |Alternate name= |Group= {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group) |location=[[Nara Prefecture]], {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, Kitsui and Ryofukuji |Coordinates={{coord|34|32|2.40|N|135|42|30.00|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |Size=Mound length: 140 m |Burial structure=(Presumed) [[Nagamochi-shaped sarcophagus]] |Artifacts= Steatite magatama with smaller magatama inside, [[haniwa]] fragments |Sub-tombs= |Period= Late [[5th century]] to early [[6th century]] |Buried individual=(Hypothetical) {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} or {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} |Imperial tomb= |Historic site=None |Tangible cultural property= |Notes= |Map= Japan Nara |Icon= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NNE |Label= Kitsui
Shiroyama Kofun |Label position= top }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Kitsui and Ryofukuji, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is one of the burial mounds forming part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group). The kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture, this large keyhole-shaped kofun dates to the late [[Kofun period]]. It underwent modifications during the [[medieval period]] when it was used as a castle (Kitsui Fort). Excavations have been conducted in the surrounding embankment and moat areas. The mound is oriented with its anterior portion facing north and measures approximately 140 meters in length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Surface features include [[fukiishi]] and haniwa fragments (cylindrical and morning glory-shaped). A moat and an embankment encircle the mound. Although the burial facility is unclear, fragments of a carved {{ill|Nagamochi-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} have been found nearby, suggesting its use in the kofun. A steatite magatama containing smaller magatama was also discovered near the constricted neck of the mound. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[5th century]] to early [[6th century]] and is considered of "great king's tomb" scale. Hypotheses suggest that it could be the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} (23rd emperor) or {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} (25th emperor), both associated with the Katsushimo District.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|title=Kofun no Hisaisha wo Suiri Suru|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2018|pages=253-260}} Stone sarcophagus fragments found nearby were determined to be made from Ryuyama stone, a type of hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa basin in Hyogo Prefecture, differing from the tuff from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijo (Nara and Osaka)|ja|二上山}}.{{PDFlink|[https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Ryuyama Stone]}} (Hyogo Prefecture official website). North of this kofun is the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳}}, which some researchers identify as the northern tomb of Emperor Buretsu, complementing this kofun as Emperor Kenzo's southern tomb.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "Domestic Largest Steatite Magatama Discovered at Kitsui Inari Kofun"] (Nara Newspaper). == History == * [[Medieval period]] to [[early modern period]]: Used as a castle (Kitsui Fort) by the Okabe clan. * [[1970]] (Showa 45): Discovery of a sarcophagus lid stone in the Hatsuda River (now displayed at Futakami Cultural Center). * [[1981]]–[[1983]]: Excavation during embankment repairs by the {{ill|Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}. * [[1985]]: Excavation during embankment repairs by the Kashiba Town Board of Education. * [[1995]] (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid stone designated as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == Mound == The mound has the following dimensions:Historical Site Explanation Board, Kashiba City Board of Education. * Mound length: 140 meters * Posterior circular portion: Diameter approx. 90 meters * Anterior portion: Width approx. 110 meters The moat surrounding the mound measures 18 meters wide on the northern side. The outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high.{{Cite book|title=Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Related Cultural Properties === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sarcophagus lid stone in front of Futakami Cultural Center
(Kashiba City-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Nagamochi-shaped sarcophagus lid stone *: Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (Archaeological Material, Designated No. 8). Located in the front yard of the Futakami Cultural Center. *: Made of hyaloclastite (Ryuyama stone) from the Kakogawa basin in Hyogo Prefecture. Measures 270 cm in length, with decorative protrusions. Found in 1970 in the Hatsuda River and presumed to have been used in this kofun. Designated March 9, 1995.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Nagamochi-shaped sarcophagus lid stone]}} (Kashiba City official website). In the surrounding area, two additional sarcophagus lid stones and a ceiling stone fragment were discovered in 1969. These are preserved at the northern end of the Amida Bridge east side.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Sarcophagus lid stones and ceiling stone fragment]}} (Kashiba City official website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lid stone and ceiling stone at Amida Bridge (Kashiba City-designated Cultural Properties) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun Explanation Board (Kashiba City Board of Education, installed in 2010) * Sarcophagus Lid Stone Explanation Board (Kashiba City Board of Education, installed in 1995) * {{Cite book|author=Heibonsha|title=Nara Prefecture Placenames|series=Japanese Historical Placenames Series Vol. 30|year=1981}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|title=The Two Great King Tombs in Katsuragi|journal=Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin|year=2011}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Kashiba City]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] * [[Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]] Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara| [[Category:dd]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:狐井城山古墳]] ... 7 KB (942 words) - Tumulus (with the front section on the right and the rear section in the back left) |Alternate name = |Group = {{ill|Mami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group) |location = {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Kitsui/Ryōfukuji area |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 32|Latitude seconds = 2.40 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 30.00 |ISO = JP-29 |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |Dimensions = Tumulus length: 140 m |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} |Artifacts = Steatite magatama beads with small beads inside, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments |Accompanying Mounds = |Construction Period = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Buried Individual = Possibly Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th) |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-NNE |Label = Kitsui Shiroyama
Kofun |Label Position = top }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group), it is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture. The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}} and {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and artifacts, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} and early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, corresponding to the middle to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal scale.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyogo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}} to {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid designated as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == Dimensions == The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * Tumulus length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Cultural Properties == === Related Cultural Properties === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyogo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::unsorted completion categories]]
