revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun |image = [[File:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (with the front section on the right and the rear section in the back left) |alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Mami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Southern Group) |location = {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Kitsui/Ryōfukuji area |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 32|coordinates = 2.40 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 30.00 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 140 m |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} |burial goods = Steatite magatama beads with small beads inside, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} fragments |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = Possibly Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th) |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-NNE |map_dot_label = Kitsui Shiroyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Southern Group), it is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture. The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世|12=draft}} and {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世|12=draft}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and burial goods, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} and early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, corresponding to the middleto late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal map_size.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyogo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳|12=draft}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世|12=draft}} to {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世|12=draft}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid Historic Site1 as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * Kofun length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Historic Site == === Related Historic Site === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyogo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (Historic Site1 as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiba]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[ja:狐井城山古墳]] [[sn:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiba]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[ja:狐井城山古墳]] [[sn:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun |image = [[File:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (with the front section on the right and the rear section in the back left) |alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Mami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Southern Group) |location = {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Kitsui/Ryōfukuji area |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 32|coordinates = 2.40 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 30.00 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 140 m |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} |burial goods = Steatite magatama beads with small beads inside, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} fragments |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = Possibly Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th) |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-NNE |map_dot_label = Kitsui Shiroyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Southern Group), it is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture. The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世|12=draft}} and {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世|12=draft}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and burial goods, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} and early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, corresponding to the middleto late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal map_size.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyogo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳|12=draft}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世|12=draft}} to {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世|12=draft}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid Historic Site1 as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * Kofun length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Historic Site == === Related Historic Site === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyogo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (Historic Site1 as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiba]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[ja:狐井城山古墳]] [[sn:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]]

Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世|12=draft}} to {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世|12=draft}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid Historic Site1 as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * Kofun length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Historic Site == === Related Historic Site === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyogo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (Historic Site1 as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiba]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:狐井城山古墳]] [[sn:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun |image = [[File:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (with the front section on the right and the rear section in the back left) |alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Mami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Southern Group) |location = {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Kitsui/Ryōfukuji area |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 32|coordinates = 2.40 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 30.00 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 140 m |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} |burial goods = Steatite magatama beads with small beads inside, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} fragments |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = Possibly Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th) |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-NNE |map_dot_label = Kitsui Shiroyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Southern Group), it is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture. The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世|12=draft}} and {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世|12=draft}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and burial goods, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} and early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, corresponding to the middleto late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-Ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal map_size.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyogo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=draft}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳|12=draft}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世|12=draft}} to {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世|12=draft}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid Historic Site1 as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * Kofun length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Historic Site == === Related Historic Site === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyogo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (Historic Site1 as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiba]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:狐井城山古墳]] [[sn:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]]

Part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Southern Group), it is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture. The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世|12=draft}} and {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世|12=draft}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and burial goods, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} and early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, corresponding to the middleto late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-Ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal map_size.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyogo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=draft}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳|12=draft}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世|12=draft}} to {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世|12=draft}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid Historic Site1 as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * Kofun length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Historic Site == === Related Historic Site === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyogo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (Historic Site1 as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiba]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:狐井城山古墳]] [[sn:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井城山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[香芝市]]狐井・良福寺 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 2.40 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 30.00 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長140m |埋葬施設 = （推定）[[長持形石棺]] |出土品 = 滑石製子持勾玉・[[埴輪]]片 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]末-[[6世紀]]初頭 |被葬者 = （一説）[[顕宗天皇]]または[[武烈天皇]] |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北北東 |ラベル = 狐井城山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''狐井城山古墳'''（きついしろやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[香芝市]]狐井・良福寺にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]（南群）を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 奈良県西部、狐井集落の南の台地上に築造された大型前方後円墳である{{Sfn|狐井城山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。[[中世]]-[[近世]]期に城砦として利用されて墳丘が改変を受けているほか、これまでに外堤・周濠部において発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北方向に向ける。墳丘長は約140メートルを測り、香芝市では最大規模になる。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]片（[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪{{Sfn|狐井城山古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}）が検出されている{{Sfn|狐井城山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。墳丘周囲には周濠・外堤が巡らされる{{Sfn|狐井城山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。埋葬施設は明らかでないが、古墳付近において刳抜式[[長持形石棺]]の破片が採集されており、本古墳における同棺の使用が推測される{{Sfn|狐井城山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。副葬品も明らかでないが、墳丘くびれ部付近で滑石製子持勾玉1点が採集されている{{Sfn|狐井城山古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 この狐井城山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]中期-後期の[[5世紀]]末葉-[[6世紀]]初頭頃の築造と推定される。被葬者は明らかでないが、当時としては大王墓級の規模の古墳になるため、[[葛下郡]]に営まれた[[顕宗天皇]]（第23代）・[[武烈天皇]]（第25代）のいずれかの真陵とする説が挙げられている[[白石太一郎]] 「古墳からみた物部氏」『古墳の被葬者を推理する（中公叢書）』 中央公論新社、2018年、pp. 253-260。。周辺で採集された石棺片は兵庫県加古川流域産の成層[[ハイアロクラスタイト]]竜山石は、かつて流紋岩質溶結凝灰岩とされてきたが、近年の調査によって流紋岩質成層ハイアロクラスタイト（水冷破砕岩）と判明している（[https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html 宝殿石（竜山石）]（兵庫県ホームページ）参照）。（竜山石）製であり、付近の[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]産凝灰岩製ではない点が当時の政治情勢を考察するうえで注意される。 なお、本古墳の北には狐井稲荷古墳が所在し、狐井城山古墳を顕宗天皇の「傍丘磐杯丘南陵」、狐井稲荷古墳を武烈天皇の「傍丘磐杯丘北陵」に比定する説が挙げられるほか、狐井稲荷古墳からは国内最大級の子持勾玉が発見された点でも注目される[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "国内最大級の子持勾玉 - 「土師氏」とかかわり? 11月28日から公開 香芝・狐井稲荷古墳"]（奈良新聞）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[中世]]-[[近世]]期、岡部氏（岡氏）が城砦（狐井塁）として使用{{Sfn|狐井城山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 * [[1970年]]（[[昭和]]45年）8月、初田川から石棺蓋石1点の発見（現在はふたかみ文化センター所在）。 * [[1981年|1981]]-[[1983年]]度（昭和56-58年度）、外堤改修工事に伴う発掘調査（[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]）。 * [[1985年]]度（昭和60年度）、外堤改修工事に伴う発掘調査（香芝町教育委員会、[[1986年]]に概報刊行）。 * [[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）3月9日、ふたかみ文化センター所在の蓋石1点が香芝市指定有形文化財に指定。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|狐井城山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}狐井城山古墳 史跡説明板。。 * 墳丘長：約140メートル * 後円部 直径：約90メートル * 前方部 幅：約110メートル 墳丘周囲に巡らされた周濠は北側で約18メートルを測り、周濠外側の外堤は幅約18メートル・高さ4.5メートルを測る{{Sfn|狐井城山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 == 文化財 == === 関連文化財 === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|ふたかみ文化センター前の
刳抜式長持形石棺蓋石
（香芝市指定文化財）}}]] * 刳抜式長持形石棺蓋石 1基 *: 香芝市指定有形文化財（考古資料、市指定第8号）。ふたかみ文化センター前庭所在。 *: 兵庫県加古川流域産の成層[[ハイアロクラスタイト]]（竜山石）製。長さ270.0センチメートルを測り、縄掛突起を付す。1970年（昭和45年）8月に初田川で発見された。狐井城山古墳での使用が推測される。1995年（平成7年）3月9日指定{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf 刳抜式長持形石棺蓋石 一基]}}（香芝市ホームページ）。。 なお周辺では、長持形石棺蓋石2基・石室天井石片1基（香芝市指定有形文化財（考古資料、市指定第16号）：天井石片は附指定）が1969年（昭和44年）に初田川から発見されたことが知られ、同石片は阿弥陀橋東詰北側に遺存する{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf 長持形石棺蓋石 二基]}}（香芝市ホームページ）。。 File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|阿弥陀橋の長持形石棺蓋石・石室天井石（香芝市指定文化財） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 狐井城山古墳 史跡説明板（香芝市教育委員会、2010年設置） * 刳抜式長持形石棺蓋石 説明板（香芝市教育委員会、1995年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=狐井城山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|狐井城山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=刊行後版（ジャパンナレッジ収録）、2006年|ref={{Harvid|狐井城山古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=木下亘|year=1989|chapter=狐井城山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|狐井城山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title=香芝町狐井城山古墳外堤試掘調査概報（2次）|publisher=香芝町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|author=|year=1984|chapter=|title=香芝町狐井城山古墳第3次発掘調査概要|publisher=香芝町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=|title=狐井城山古墳外堤 -昭和60年度第4次発掘調査概報-|publisher=香芝町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=白石太一郎|authorlink=白石太一郎|year=2011|chapter=葛城の二つの大王墓 -顕宗陵と武烈陵の問題-|title=大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館館報 14|publisher=[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=奥田尚|title=香芝市狐井城山古墳付近の石棺・石室材|date=2016-02|publisher=古代学研究会|journal=古代学研究|volume=|number=208|pages=43-49|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=大平茂|title=子持勾玉の祭祀儀礼 -土師氏から三輪氏へ-|date=2021-7-1|publisher=大神神社|journal=大美和|volume=|number=141|pages=12-18|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun}} * [[顕宗天皇]] * [[武烈天皇]] {{デフォルトソート:きついしろやまこふん}} Kofun (with the front section on the right and the rear section in the back left) |alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Mami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} (Southern Group) |location = {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Kitsui/Ryōfukuji area |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 32|coordinates = 2.40 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 30.00 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 140 m |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = Steatite magatama beads with small beads inside, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Interred_individual = Possibly Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th) |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-NNE |map_dot_label = Kitsui Shiroyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} (Southern Group), it is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture. The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}} and {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and burial goods, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} and early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, corresponding to the middleto late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-Ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal map_size.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyogo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世}} to {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid Historic Site1 as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * Kofun length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Historic Site == === Related Historic Site === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyogo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (Historic Site1 as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiba]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:狐井城山古墳]] [[sn:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|狐井城山古墳|version=99995792|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世|12=draft}} to {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世|12=draft}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid Historic Site1 as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * Kofun length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Historic Site == === Related Historic Site === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyogo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (Historic Site1 as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|狐井城山古墳|version=99995792|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiba]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]] [[ja:狐井城山古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun |image = [[File:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (with the front section on the right and the rear section in the back left) |alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Mami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft}} (Southern Group) |location = {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft}}, Kitsui/Ryōfukuji area |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 32|coordinates = 2.40 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 30.00 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 140 m |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft}} |burial goods = Steatite magatama beads with small beads inside, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft}} fragments |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft}} |Interred_individual = Possibly Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th) |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-NNE |map_dot_label = Kitsui Shiroyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市|qq=draft}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft}} (Southern Group), it is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture. The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世|qq=draft}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and burial goods, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft}} and early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft}}, corresponding to the middleto late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-Ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal map_size.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyogo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳|qq=draft}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世|qq=draft}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid Historic Site1 as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * Kofun length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Historic Site == === Related Historic Site === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyogo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (Historic Site1 as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|qq=draft}} {{citation needed|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|狐井城山古墳|version=99995792|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiba]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]] [[ja:狐井城山古墳]]

Kofun (with the front section on the right and the rear section in the back left) |alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Mami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group) |location = {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiba, Nara|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, Kitsui/Ryōfukuji area |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 32|coordinates = 2.40 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 30.00 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} |area = Kofun length: 140 m |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} |burial goods = Steatite magatama beads with small beads inside, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} fragments |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} |Interred_individual = Possibly Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th) |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-NNE |map_dot_label = Kitsui Shiroyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|ja|香芝市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiba, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group), it is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture. The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medieval Japan|qq=}} and {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early modern period|qq=}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and burial goods, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}} and early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}}, corresponding to the middleto late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-Ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal map_size.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyogo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Inari Kofun|qq=}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medieval Japan|qq=}} to {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early modern period|qq=}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid Historic Site1 as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * Kofun length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Historic Site == === Related Historic Site === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyogo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (Historic Site1 as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kenzo|qq=}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Buretsu|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|狐井城山古墳|version=99995792|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiba]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]] [[ja:狐井城山古墳]]

The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medieval Japan|qq=}} and {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early modern period|qq=}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Inari Kofun|qq=}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyōgo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and burial goods, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}} and early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}}, corresponding to the middleto late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-Ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal map_size.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyōgo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Inari Kofun|qq=}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Medieval Japan|ja|中世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medieval Japan|qq=}} to {{ill|Early modern period|ja|近世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early modern period|qq=}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid Historic Site1 as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * Kofun length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Historic Site == === Related Historic Site === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyōgo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (Historic Site1 as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kenzo|qq=}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Buretsu|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|狐井城山古墳|version=99995792|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiba]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kitsune-i Shiroyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]] [[ja:狐井城山古墳]]
