revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]][[ja:狐井城山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]][[ja:狐井城山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]][[ja:狐井城山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun |image = [[File:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (with the front section on the right and the rear section in the back left) |alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Mami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group) |location = {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|en|Kashiba, Nara|WD=Q696643|lt=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Kitsui/Ryōfukuji area |coordinates = {{Coord|34.534|N|135.708333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 140 m |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} |burial goods = Steatite magatama beads with small beads inside, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} fragments |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} to early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} |Interred_individual = Possibly Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th) |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-NNE |map_dot_label = Kitsui Shiroyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|en|Kashiba, Nara|WD=Q696643|lt=Kashiba, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group), it is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture. The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=Medieval Japan}} and {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|WD=Q5308718|lt=Early modern period}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and burial goods, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} and early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, corresponding to the middleto late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-Ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal map_size.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyōgo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=Medieval Japan}} to {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|WD=Q5308718|lt=Early modern period}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid Historic Site1 as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * Kofun length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Historic Site == === Related Historic Site === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyōgo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (Historic Site1 as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzō|en|Emperor Kenzō|WD=Q360966|lt=Emperor Kenzo}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|WD=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|狐井城山古墳|version=99995792|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:狐井城山古墳]] {{Commons category|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q60988838}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=Medieval Japan|1=Middle Ages}} to {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|WD=Q5308718|lt=Early modern period|1=Early modern period}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid Historic Site1 as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * Kofun length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Historic Site == === Related Historic Site === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyōgo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (Historic Site1 as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzō|en|Emperor Kenzō|WD=Q360966|lt=Emperor Kenzo|1=Emperor Kenzō}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|WD=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu|1=Emperor Buretsu}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|狐井城山古墳|version=99995792|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:狐井城山古墳]] {{Commons category|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q60988838}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

Kofun (with the front section on the right and the rear section in the back left) |alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Mami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group) |location = {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|en|Kashiba, Nara|WD=Q696643|lt=Kashiba, Nara|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Kitsui/Ryōfukuji area |coordinates = {{Coord|34.534|N|135.708333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 140 m |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} |burial goods = Steatite magatama beads with small beads inside, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} to early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} |Interred_individual = Possibly Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th) |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-NNE |map_dot_label = Kitsui Shiroyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|en|Kashiba, Nara|WD=Q696643|lt=Kashiba, Nara|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group), it is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture. The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=Medieval Japan|1=Middle Ages}} and {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|WD=Q5308718|lt=Early modern period|1=Early modern period}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and burial goods, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} and early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, corresponding to the middleto late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-Ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal map_size.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyōgo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=Medieval Japan|1=Middle Ages}} to {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|WD=Q5308718|lt=Early modern period|1=Early modern period}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid Historic Site1 as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * Kofun length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Historic Site == === Related Historic Site === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyōgo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (Historic Site1 as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). [[co:commons:Category:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]][[ja:狐井城山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]][[ja:狐井城山古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]][[ja:狐井城山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun |image = [[File:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (with the front section on the right and the rear section in the back left) |alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Mami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group) |location = {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|en|Kashiba, Nara|WD=Q696643|lt=Kashiba, Nara|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Kitsui/Ryōfukuji area |coordinates = {{Coord|34.534|N|135.708333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 140 m |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} |burial goods = Steatite magatama beads with small beads inside, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} to early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} |Interred_individual = Possibly Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th) |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-NNE |map_dot_label = Kitsui Shiroyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|en|Kashiba, Nara|WD=Q696643|lt=Kashiba, Nara|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group), it is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture. The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=Medieval Japan|1=Middle Ages}} and {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|WD=Q5308718|lt=Early modern period|1=Early modern period}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and burial goods, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} and early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, corresponding to the middleto late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-Ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal map_size.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyōgo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=Medieval Japan|1=Middle Ages}} to {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|WD=Q5308718|lt=Early modern period|1=Early modern period}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid Historic Site1 as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * Kofun length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Historic Site == === Related Historic Site === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyōgo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (Historic Site1 as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzō|en|Emperor Kenzō|WD=Q360966|lt=Emperor Kenzo|1=Emperor Kenzō}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|WD=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu|1=Emperor Buretsu}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|狐井城山古墳|version=99995792|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:狐井城山古墳]] {{Commons category|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q60988838}} [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and burial goods, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} and early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, corresponding to the middleto late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-Ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal map_size.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyōgo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|qid=Q12554|lt=Medieval Japan|1=Middle Ages}} to {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|qid=Q5308718|lt=Early modern period|1=Early modern period}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid Historic Site1 as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * Kofun length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Historic Site == === Related Historic Site === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyōgo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (Historic Site1 as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzō|en|Emperor Kenzō|qid=Q360966|lt=Emperor Kenzo|1=Emperor Kenzō}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|qid=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu|1=Emperor Buretsu}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|狐井城山古墳|version=99995792|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:狐井城山古墳]] {{Commons category|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q60988838}} [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]][[ja:狐井城山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun]][[ja:狐井城山古墳]] [[ja:狐井城山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpōkōenfun]] |name = Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun |image = [[File:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (with the front section on the right and the rear section in the back left) |alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Mami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group) |location = {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|en|Kashiba, Nara|qid=Q696643|lt=Kashiba, Nara|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Kitsui/Ryōfukuji area |coordinates = {{Coord|34.534|N|135.708333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 140 m |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} |burial goods = Steatite magatama beads with small beads inside, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} to early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} |Interred_individual = Possibly Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th) |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-NNE |map_dot_label = Kitsui Shiroyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|en|Kashiba, Nara|qid=Q696643|lt=Kashiba, Nara|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group), it is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture. The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|qid=Q12554|lt=Medieval Japan|1=Middle Ages}} and {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|qid=Q5308718|lt=Early modern period|1=Early modern period}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and burial goods, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} and early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, corresponding to the middleto late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-Ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal map_size.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyōgo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|qid=Q12554|lt=Medieval Japan|1=Middle Ages}} to {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|qid=Q5308718|lt=Early modern period|1=Early modern period}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid Historic Site1 as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * Kofun length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Historic Site == === Related Historic Site === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyōgo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (Historic Site1 as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzō|en|Emperor Kenzō|qid=Q360966|lt=Emperor Kenzo|1=Emperor Kenzō}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|qid=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu|1=Emperor Buretsu}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|狐井城山古墳|version=99995792|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:狐井城山古墳]] {{Commons category|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q60988838}} [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpōkōenfun]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] |name = Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun |image = [[File:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (with the front section on the right and the rear section in the back left) |alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Mami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group) |location = {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|en|Kashiba, Nara|qid=Q696643|lt=Kashiba, Nara|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Kitsui/Ryōfukuji area |coordinates = {{Coord|34.534|N|135.708333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} |area = Kofun length: 140 m |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} |burial goods = Steatite magatama beads with small beads inside, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} to early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} |Interred_individual = Possibly Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th) |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-NNE |map_dot_label = Kitsui Shiroyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun''' (狐井城山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} located in {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|en|Kashiba, Nara|qid=Q696643|lt=Kashiba, Nara|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group), it is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a plateau south of the Kitsui settlement in western Nara Prefecture. The mound underwent modifications during the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|qid=Q12554|lt=Medieval Japan|1=Middle Ages}} and {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|qid=Q5308718|lt=Early modern period|1=Early modern period}}, when it was repurposed as a fort. Excavations have been conducted in the embankments and moats surrounding the kofun. The tumulus, oriented to the north, measures approximately 140 meters in total length, making it the largest kofun in Kashiba City. Haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, as well as fukiishi (covering stones), have been recovered from the mound's surface. The tumulus is surrounded by moats and embankments.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, fragments of a hewn {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were found near the kofun, suggesting its use in this burial. A magatama bead made of steatite was also collected near the narrowing of the mound.{{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha, later edition)|2006}} Based on its structure and burial goods, the kofun is estimated to have been constructed between the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} and early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, corresponding to the middleto late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. The burial is believed to be of a high-Ranking individual, potentially Emperor Kenzo (23rd) or Emperor Buretsu (25th), as the tumulus is of royal map_size.{{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} The stone fragments, made of Hyaloclastite from the Kakogawa region in Hyōgo Prefecture (commonly called Tatsuyama stone), are noteworthy for political implications, as they are not sourced from the nearby {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. To the north, the {{ill|Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja|狐井稲荷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsui Inari Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is located, and it has been suggested that these two tumuli correspond to the "傍丘磐杯丘南陵" (southern mound) and "傍丘磐杯丘北陵" (northern mound) mentioned in historical texts. Kitsui Inari Kofun is particularly notable for the discovery of one of the largest magatama beads in Japan.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20201126091432.html "One of Japan's Largest Magatama Beads - Linked to Haji Clan?"] (Nara Shimbun). == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|qid=Q12554|lt=Medieval Japan|1=Middle Ages}} to {{ill|Early modern period|en|Early modern period|qid=Q5308718|lt=Early modern period|1=Early modern period}}: Used as a fort by the Okabe clan. * 1970 (Shōwa 45): A fragment of a sarcophagus lid was discovered in Hatsuta River.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Nagamochi-type Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). * 1981–1983 (Shōwa 56–58): Archaeological investigations conducted during embankment repair projects. * 1985 (Shōwa 60): Further excavation during embankment repairs. * 1995 (Heisei 7): Sarcophagus lid Historic Site1 as a Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property. == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * Kofun length: Approximately 140 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 90 meters * Front section width: Approximately 110 meters The surrounding moat measures approximately 18 meters wide on the northern side, and the outer embankment is about 18 meters wide and 4.5 meters high. == Historic Site == === Related Historic Site === [[File:Piece of Sekkan in front of Futakami Museum.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid
(Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property)}}]] * Hewn Nagamochi-type sarcophagus lid (1 piece) ** Designated as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property (archaeological artifact, No. 8). Located in the garden of Futakami Cultural Center. ** Made of Tatsuyama stone (Hyaloclastite from Hyōgo Prefecture). Measures 270 cm in length, featuring rope-like projections. Discovered in Hatsuta River in 1970 (Shōwa 45). Other related discoveries include two sarcophagus lids and a piece of a chamber ceiling stone (Historic Site1 as Kashiba City Tangible Cultural Property No. 16), found in Hatsuta River in 1969 (Shōwa 44).{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai016.pdf Description of Sarcophagus Lids and Ceiling Stone]}} (Kashiba City website). File:Piece of Sekkan at Amidabashi, Kashiba-2.jpg|Sarcophagus lids and ceiling stone near Amida Bridge (Kashiba City Cultural Property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun explanatory board (Kashiba City Board of Education, 2010). * {{Harvnb|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} * {{Harvnb|Shiraishi|2018}} * {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000000/99/bunka-zai008.pdf Description of Hewn Sarcophagus Lid]}} (Kashiba City website). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Kenzō|en|Emperor Kenzō|qid=Q360966|lt=Emperor Kenzo|1=Emperor Kenzō}} * {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|qid=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu|1=Emperor Buretsu}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|狐井城山古墳|version=99995792|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q60988838|co|commons:Category:Kitsui Shiroyama Kofun|ja|狐井城山古墳}} [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]
